description:
File: secretx-tpm2/src/lib.rs

Five private helper functions lack doc comments. The project standard requires doc comments on everything, including module-private items.

Locations:
- line 178: fn parse_hex_u32(s: &str, label: &str) -> Result<u32, SecretError>
- line 185: fn validate_nv_index(index: u32) -> Result<(), SecretError>
- line 194: fn validate_persistent_handle(handle: u32) -> Result<(), SecretError>
- line 203: fn open_context(tcti: &str) -> Result<Context, SecretError>
- line 520: fn algorithm_label(algo: SigningAlgorithm) -> &'static str

What goes wrong as-is: maintainers must read the function body to understand intent. For validation functions, the doc comment should state the valid range. For open_context, it should note the fresh-context-per-call design decision.

Fix: add a one-line /// doc comment to each. The names are reasonably self-documenting, so a brief summary suffices.
title: Add doc comments to private helper functions
